prompt: Hook visual: Not all volcanoes erupt the same way—what's the secret?
Context visual: Shield volcanoes have gentle slopes, formed by low-viscosity lava that flows easily. Think Mauna Loa in Hawaii.
Escalation visual: Stratovolcanoes, like Mount St. Helens, are steeper and more explosive, due to thicker, more viscous lava.
